items.title: This 120W car charger with dual 60W Type-C and two USB-A ports just struck $17 (Reg. $30)
items.summary:
JOYROOM 120W Dual USB-A/Type-C Car Charger

Buy for $17 (Reg. $30)

What we love

Outfitted with two USB-A and two Type-C ports, there are enough outputs here to keep a family of four topped off while on the go. It can dish out a total of 120W, with both USB-C slots able to independently reach 60W.

items.title: Fanttik’s slim V8 APEX vacuum with USB-C charging keeps your vehicle, office, and more tidy for $66 (Reg. $100)
items.summary:
Fanttik Slim V8 APEX Car Vacuum

Buy for $66 (Reg. $100)

What we love

I personally find that the easier I make it to keep things clean, the more often I will actually do it. That’s why I recommend keeping a vacuum like this in your vehicle, office, or well, really anywhere that can get dirty and often overlooked. In typical Fanttik fashion, it charges via USB-C to make things feel handy and modern.

items.title: Need more power? This affordable 12-in-1 surge protector is only $14 right now

TROND 12-in-1 USB-C Surge Protector

Buy for $14 (Reg. $19)

What we love

Outfitted with 8 AC outlets, one USB-A, and three Type-C ports, this surge protector really packs a punch if you ask me. Especially at $14 Prime shipped. I personally have at least three TROND surge protectors in my home and have zero complaints.

items.title: It’s back – Acer’s 240W USB4/Thunderbolt cable can be yours for $16
items.summary:
Acer 240W USB4/Thunderbolt Cable

Buy for $16 (Reg. $20)

What we love

When this deal comes around, we cover it. While it may not be Thunderbolt 5, it gets as close as possible while remaining on the affordable side and being made by a big brand. It features 240W charging as well as USB4 and Thunderbolt 4 data transfer speeds at 40Gb/s.

SK Hynix opens up 14% at $170 after raising $26.5B in the largest ever US market debut by a foreign company  —  SK Hynix opened at $170 on the Nasdaq on Friday, rising about 14%, as U.S. investors jumped at the opportunity to get a stake in South Korea's second most valuable company.

items.title: Score up to $464 increased savings on Lectric’s Xpress2, Xpeak2, and XP4 750 e-bikes starting from $1,299
items.summary: Lectric is going back to celebrating America’s 250th Anniversary with up to $464 of increased savings offers over the previous July 4th deals for three of its popular e-bikes – the XP4 750, Xpress2, and Xpeak2 models. Getting its second-biggest bundle since launching in May is the Xpress2 Long-Range Step-Thru Cruiser e-bike and its counterpart XPress2 Long-Range High-Step Commuter e-bike – both of which are priced for $1,399 shipped with $345 FREE bundles. Already building up quite the popularity since hitting the market two months ago, these bundles would normally fetch $1,744 at full price without the savings, with today’s bundle only beaten out by the $385 collection of gear it received during the brand’s Prime Day Sale event in June. You can also find ongoing deals for the brand’s other models on the landing page here.

items.summary:
FiNESTONE CRNS-PRO Titanium Apple Watch Band

Buy for $35 (Reg. $80)

What we love

We touched on this deal about a week ago, but we’re featuring it again because, well, we think it’s truly a solid find. You’re getting a grade 2 titanium band with tool-free adjustment for just $35, which is certainly an impressive price point from where I’m standing.

items.title: This minimalist leather wallet is yours for only $8.50 Prime shipped
items.summary:
Travelambo Slim Leather Minimalist Wallet

Buy for $8.50 (Reg. $10)

Despite it’s small size, this design boasts six card slots so you can still get pretty much everything you might need in there.

items.title: Save a whopping $850 today on Lenovo’s loaded Legion Pro 7i RTX 5080 OLED gaming laptop with 32GB RAM/2TB SSD
items.summary: B&H is now offering Lenovo’s 16-inch Legion Pro 7i gaming laptop with an RTX 5080 GPU for $2,649 shipped. The retailer has this configuration listed with a $3,499 regular price, so today’s offer knocks $850 off to make it easier on your wallet. Lenovo, meanwhile, has listed the 1TB SSD variant of this laptop itself at $4,614, meaning you’re potentially looking at significantly higher savings than B&H’s advertised discount. Either way, $2,649 is a pretty solid price for one of the most powerful configs out there right now with an RTX 5080 GPU, especially with a spacious 2TB drive included. Head below for more details.

items.summary:
